rules.rule_group: Part A About This Plan
rules.rule_text: Willoughby DCP provides guidelines and controls for developments within the Willoughby local government area. It repeals DCP 2006 pursuant to section 16 of the Environmental Planning and Assessment Regulation 2021 and supports the objectives and planning provisions in Willoughby LEP.

rules.rule_group: Part A Aim
rules.rule_text: The aim of Part A is to give an overview of the different sections of Willoughby DCP.

rules.rule_group: Part A Objectives
rules.rule_text: Part A: (a) advises when a development application is needed and the supporting information required; (b) explains how this plan relates to other state and local plans, policies, and guidelines; (c) details the other parts of Willoughby DCP.

rules.rule_group: Part A Land Covered
rules.rule_text: This plan applies to all land and development within the City of Willoughby local government area.

rules.rule_group: Part A Existing Use Rights
rules.rule_text: The existing use provisions under Division 4.11 of the EPA Act 1979 and Part 5 of the EP Regulations 2000 apply to any development that is not a permissible land use within a particular zone. This includes dwellings not permissible in R3 or R4 zones. An application for change of use, alterations, or rebuilding of a non-conforming existing use must address standards for that type of development otherwise permissible in the zone.

rules.rule_group: Part A BASIX SEPP
rules.rule_text: An application for residential accommodation must include a BASIX certificate. Willoughby City Council cannot approve a DA for residential development exceeding $50,000, or a swimming pool/spa exceeding 40,000 litres, if it does not achieve the minimum BASIX score.

rules.rule_group: Part B Aim
rules.rule_text: Part B aims to provide a consistent and equitable approach to residential development outcomes. It incorporates relevant state planning policies and current best practice provisions into local planning controls for the City of Willoughby.

rules.rule_group: Part B Checklist for DAs
rules.rule_text: A development application for residential development must include the relevant compliance checklist (Attachment 1 for R2 minor development; Attachment 2 for manor houses, attached dwellings and multi dwelling housing in R3; Attachment 3 for residential flat buildings, shop top housing and mixed use developments of 3+ storeys and 4+ dwellings).

rules.rule_text: To ensure new allotments have access to services and facilities and that subdivided land has the capacity for the proposed development on individual lots. [controls in image files – not extracted]

rules.rule_group: Part B Private Open Space – Notes
rules.rule_text: If it is necessary to locate private open space at the front of the property due to site constraints, the relationship to the streetscape should be considered in terms of planting, fencing, privacy, noise, and security. Private open space must not include driveways, turning areas or car spaces.

rules.rule_group: Part B Minor Developments – Definition
rules.rule_text: Minor residential developments include construction of and alterations and additions to dwelling houses, dual occupancies, and other minor forms of residential accommodation (see attached images). A boarding house must be maintained and operated as a single entity; strata subdivision is not permitted. An authorised officer may determine a proposal is 'major' development depending on size, scale, and likely impacts.

rules.development_sub_category: Controls for Development in the R2 Zone
rules.rule_group: Part B Controls for Development in the R2 Zone
rules.rule_title: Controls for Development in the R2 Zone
rules.rule_text: This plan adopts the relevant controls from State Environmental Planning Policy (Exempt and Complying Development Codes) 2008 (Codes SEPP) for development in the R2 zone, including Housing Code (Part 3), Low Rise Housing Diversity Code (Part 3B) and Housing Alterations Code (Part 4). Notable exceptions are the floor space ratio (FSR), landscaped area, number of car parking spaces, and certain additional heritage controls. Any development for attached or detached dual occupancies must satisfy the objectives and design criteria in the Low Rise Housing Diversity Design Guide (DPIE, July 2020).
rules.applies_to: Controls for Development in the R2 Zone

rules.development_sub_category: Controls for Landscaping – Minimum Landscaped Area Table
rules.rule_group: Part B Controls for Landscaping – Minimum Landscaped Area Table
rules.rule_title: Controls for Landscaping – Minimum Landscaped Area Table
rules.rule_text: Minimum landscaped area: Under 200m² = 0.25 × site area; 200–400m² = (0.35 × site area) − 20; 401–600m² = (0.6 × site area) − 120; 601–1000m² = (0.525 × site area) − 75; 1001–1500m² = (0.6 × site area) − 150; Over 1500m² = 0.5 × site area. Minimum landscaped area includes all deep soil zones and soft landscaped areas (minimum dimension 1m). Deep soil zones are free of sub-ground structures. Soft landscaped areas comprise a minimum depth of 600mm.
rules.applies_to: Controls for Landscaping – Minimum Landscaped Area Table

rules.development_sub_category: Controls for Landscaping – Front Setback Landscaping
rules.rule_group: Part B Controls for Landscaping – Front Setback Landscaping
rules.rule_title: Controls for Landscaping – Front Setback Landscaping
rules.rule_text: 70% of the area forward of the building line to the primary road where a site has a width, measured at the building line, of at least 18m. 50% of the area forward of the building line to the primary road where a site has a width, measured at the building line, of less than 18m.
rules.applies_to: Controls for Landscaping – Front Setback Landscaping

rules.rule_group: Part B Controls for Ancillary Structures
rules.rule_text: This plan adopts the development standards in the Housing Code (Codes SEPP, Part 3) for attached and detached balconies, decks, patios, terraces and verandahs located at the side and rear of a dwelling. Greater emphasis is placed on maintaining visual privacy to living areas and private open spaces than for non-living rooms including bedrooms, stairways and bathrooms.

rules.rule_group: Part B Car Parking and Vehicular Access
rules.rule_text: Except for the number of car parking spaces, this plan adopts the requirements for car parking and vehicle access in the Housing Code (Codes SEPP, Part 3). The required number of car parking spaces are provided in Part F. A new driveway crossing may be required if the proposal involves a change to the vehicle access arrangements.

rules.development_sub_category: Earthworks, Retaining Walls, Drainage, Protection of Trees
rules.rule_group: Part B Earthworks, Retaining Walls, Drainage, Protection of Trees
rules.rule_title: Earthworks, Retaining Walls, Drainage, Protection of Trees
rules.rule_text: This plan adopts the requirements of the Housing Code (Codes SEPP, Part 3) for earthworks, retaining walls, drainage, protection of walls, and protection of trees. All earthworks and/or construction of retaining walls must consider Clause 6.2 of Willoughby LEP. All drainage works must consider Part I (Stormwater Management). Removal or replacement planting of trees must consider Part G (Vegetation Management).
rules.applies_to: Earthworks, Retaining Walls, Drainage, Protection of Trees

rules.rule_group: Part B Fences
rules.rule_text: This plan adopts the requirements of the Housing Code (Codes SEPP, Part 3) for the construction of fences. Any variation must demonstrate the proposed fence meets the performance criteria (detailed controls in image files). [controls in image files – not extracted]
rules.extraction_note: variation criteria in image files – not extractable

rules.rule_group: Part B Swimming Pools
rules.rule_text: This plan adopts the requirements of the Housing Code (Codes SEPP, Part 3) for the construction or installation of a swimming pool and/or spa. Any variation must show the swimming pool will not have significant impacts on adjoining and nearby residential properties.

rules.rule_group: Part B Major Developments – Definition
rules.rule_text: Major development includes boarding houses exceeding 300m² or 12 persons, manor houses, multi dwelling housing, attached dwellings, residential flat buildings, seniors housing, build-to-rent housing, and the residential component of shop top housing and mixed use developments. Redevelopment is generally 'substantial' if it results in more than a 10% increase in gross floor area.

rules.development_sub_category: Site Area and Lot Dimensions – Major
rules.rule_group: Part B Site Area and Lot Dimensions – Major
rules.rule_title: Site Area and Lot Dimensions – Major
rules.rule_text: Under Clause 6.10 of Willoughby LEP, the minimum lot size for manor houses, attached dwellings, multi dwelling housing and residential flat buildings in the R3 and R4 zones is 1100m². Allotments should be amalgamated to achieve the minimum lot size. Council may consider a variation if: the development is on an existing allotment that cannot be consolidated; remaining allotments are not left isolated; performance criteria are satisfied; and neighbouring landowners are unwilling to consolidate. Written evidence of consultation and an independent valuation must be provided. Any variation requires a written submission under Clause 4.6 of Willoughby LEP.
rules.applies_to: Site Area and Lot Dimensions – Major

rules.rule_group: Part B Street Frontage
rules.rule_text: The minimum street frontage is 27m. This is to ensure vehicles can enter and leave a site in a forward direction, and adequate landscaped areas are provided along the streetscape.

rules.rule_group: Part B Adaptable Housing, Access and Mobility
rules.rule_text: Adaptable housing must comply with Australian Standard AS 4299–1995. A minimum number of adaptable dwellings (Class C standard minimum; Class A and B encouraged) must be provided for all manor houses, attached dwellings, multi dwelling housing, residential flat buildings, and residential components of shop top housing and mixed use developments. Communal areas must provide a continuous accessible path of travel. Development must comply with the Disability (Access to Premises – Buildings) Standards 2010.

rules.rule_group: Part B Energy Efficiency
rules.rule_text: All major developments are required to comply with the relevant provisions of Part J (Building Sustainability) of this plan.

rules.rule_group: Part B Bicycle and Car Parking
rules.rule_text: All developments with 20 dwellings/apartments or more must submit a travel demand management plan. Refer to Part F (Transport and Parking Management) for parking requirements.

rules.rule_group: Part B Water Management and Conservation
rules.rule_text: All major developments must provide onsite detention systems to capture and detain stormwater runoff for recycling and to mitigate flooding impacts. The DA must include details of stormwater management. See Part I for more information.

rules.rule_group: Part B Urban Heat – Major
rules.rule_text: Buildings should use external materials, façade elements and glazing to limit solar reflectivity. This may include retractable shade structures, green walls and roofs, and/or rooftop solar panels. Roofs must achieve specified Solar Reflectance Index (SRI) values (see image tables). Plans must illustrate SRI values of all roof surfaces; SRI values not required for areas where PV panels are mounted flat. [controls in image files – not extracted]
rules.extraction_note: SRI value tables in image files | Supplemented by numeric controls in merged rules.

rules.rule_group: Part B Waste Management
rules.rule_text: All major residential developments must comply with the Waste Management Technical Guide and Development Controls by North Sydney Regional Organisation of Councils for multi dwelling housing, residential flat buildings and mixed-use developments, covering internal waste storage, bin storage, carting routes and collection vehicle access.

rules.rule_group: Part B Safety by Design
rules.rule_text: All major developments must include safety and security measures to prevent criminal activity, including high visibility to front entries, careful siting of shrubs, and lighting of pathways or hidden spaces. The DA must include details of design and safety measures.

rules.rule_group: Part B Utility Structures
rules.rule_text: The design of the development must have adequate provision for utility structures such as substations and water main boosters (detailed controls in image files). [controls in image files – not extracted]

rules.rule_group: Part B Undergrounding of Services
rules.rule_text: All services, including overhead electricity wires, are to be located underground for major development, including publicly owned land immediately outside the development site. Any proposed variation must include a written request justifying why the requirement cannot be satisfied; if cost-prohibitive, a detailed cost report from a registered quantity surveyor is required.

rules.rule_group: Part B Site Coverage – Residential Flat Buildings
rules.rule_text: Site coverage for residential flat buildings must progressively decrease as building height increases (specific percentage controls in image files). [controls in image files – not extracted]
rules.extraction_note: coverage percentage table in image files | Supplemented by numeric controls in merged rules.

rules.rule_group: Part B Building Height
rules.rule_text: Maximum height of buildings is controlled by Clauses 4.3 and 4.3A of Willoughby LEP. Generally: 9m ≈ 3 storeys; 12m ≈ 4 storeys; 15m ≈ 5 storeys; 18m ≈ 6 storeys. Section 2C of the Apartment Design Guide details matters to consider.

rules.rule_group: Part B Floor Space Ratio
rules.rule_text: Maximum FSR is controlled by Clauses 4.4 and 4.4A of Willoughby LEP. FSR is the theoretical maximum capacity; it may not always be achievable due to other controls or site constraints. Section 2D of the Apartment Design Guide details matters to consider.

rules.rule_group: Part B Setbacks – Residential Flat Buildings
rules.rule_text: Street and side/rear setback controls align with Sections 2G and 2H of the Apartment Design Guide. Car parking structures are not permitted within the front setback. The total required setback applies to all floors above the third floor for side and rear setbacks. Balconies and verandahs not more than 1m above finished ground level, and unroofed terraces/steps/ramps not more than 1m in height, are permitted within side and rear setbacks.

rules.rule_group: Part B Open Space
rules.rule_text: Controls for open space of 3+ storey, 4+ dwelling developments align with Sections 3D, 3E and 4O of the Apartment Design Guide (communal and public open space, deep soil zones, landscape design). Specific numerical controls in image files. [controls in image files – not extracted]
rules.extraction_note: numerical controls in image files | Supplemented by numeric controls in merged rules.

rules.rule_group: Part B Secondary Dwellings
rules.rule_text: Secondary dwellings are addressed in Part B Section 5.1. Specific controls are detailed (partially in image files). [controls in image files – not extracted]

rules.rule_group: Part B Bed and Breakfast Accommodation
rules.rule_text: Bed and breakfast accommodation is only permitted in the R2 zone.

rules.rule_group: Part B Boarding Houses
rules.rule_text: Boarding houses providing affordable housing in an accessible area may be carried out with consent under the Housing SEPP, subject to compliance with development standards. A plan of management must be submitted covering topics including (detailed in image files). A copy must be provided to all residents and displayed prominently at all times. A boarding house must be maintained as a single entity; subdivision is not permitted. [controls in image files – not extracted]
rules.extraction_note: plan of management topics in image files

rules.rule_group: Part B Build-to-Rent Housing
rules.rule_text: Build-to-rent housing is permissible with consent under the Housing SEPP. Applications will be assessed against performance criteria and controls in Part B and the Apartment Design Guide. If development standards under the Housing SEPP are complied with, Council cannot apply more onerous standards.

rules.rule_group: Part B Affordable Housing
rules.rule_text: Affordable housing is to be provided in accordance with Willoughby LEP 2012 and Willoughby City Council Affordable Housing Policy.

rules.development_sub_category: Child Care Centres in Residential Areas
rules.rule_group: Part B Child Care Centres in Residential Areas
rules.rule_title: Child Care Centres in Residential Areas
rules.rule_text: Child care centres are a commercial use permissible in residential zones. Section 7.3 of Part D applies. Additional controls for child care facilities in residential areas are specified (detailed controls in image files). [controls in image files – not extracted]
rules.applies_to: Child Care Centres in Residential Areas

rules.development_sub_category: 18–26 Eric Road and 487–503 Pacific Highway Artarmon – Rear Setback
rules.rule_group: Part B 18–26 Eric Road and 487–503 Pacific Highway Artarmon – Rear Setback
rules.rule_title: 18–26 Eric Road and 487–503 Pacific Highway Artarmon – Rear Setback
rules.rule_text: All new developments at 18–26 Eric Road and 487–503 Pacific Highway, Artarmon must provide a minimum rear setback of 12m to provide adequate separation and landscaped area and minimise privacy and solar access impacts.
rules.applies_to: 18–26 Eric Road and 487–503 Pacific Highway Artarmon – Rear Setback

rules.rule_group: Part B 33–35 Garland Road Naremburn – Pedestrian Access
rules.rule_text: Any new development at 33–35 Garland Road must dedicate a 1.5m wide strip of land on the western side of No. 33 and the eastern side of No. 35 to achieve a 3m wide pedestrian pathway to Hallstrom Park. For FSR calculation purposes, the total floor area may include the area of land dedicated as public open space.

rules.development_sub_category: Crowley Lane Naremburn – Deed of Agreement
rules.rule_group: Part B Crowley Lane Naremburn – Deed of Agreement
rules.rule_title: Crowley Lane Naremburn – Deed of Agreement
rules.rule_text: New developments bordering Crowley Lane between Nolan Crescent and Park Road must enter into a deed of agreement to purchase and consolidate half the width of the laneway for the full width of the development site.
rules.applies_to: Crowley Lane Naremburn – Deed of Agreement

rules.development_sub_category: 1–7 Hotham Street and 2 Wyvern Avenue Chatswood – Vehicular Access
rules.rule_group: Part B 1–7 Hotham Street and 2 Wyvern Avenue Chatswood – Vehicular Access
rules.rule_title: 1–7 Hotham Street and 2 Wyvern Avenue Chatswood – Vehicular Access
rules.rule_text: All vehicle access is to be provided from Hotham Street to ensure safe egress by diverting vehicle access away from the intersection of Wyvern Avenue and the Pacific Highway.
rules.applies_to: 1–7 Hotham Street and 2 Wyvern Avenue Chatswood – Vehicular Access

rules.development_sub_category: 1–7 Hotham Street and 2 Wyvern Avenue Chatswood – Drainage/Flooding
rules.rule_group: Part B 1–7 Hotham Street and 2 Wyvern Avenue Chatswood – Drainage/Flooding
rules.rule_title: 1–7 Hotham Street and 2 Wyvern Avenue Chatswood – Drainage/Flooding
rules.rule_text: Any new development must submit a flood study to identify the existing flood extent and show the development will not adversely affect adjoining properties. No structures other than suspended/elevated vehicular crossings are permitted over the identified flood extent. Under Clause 6.10 of Willoughby LEP, the minimum site area for any future development is 3,500m². Any future development must dedicate a drainage easement in favour of, and at no cost to, Willoughby City Council.
rules.applies_to: 1–7 Hotham Street and 2 Wyvern Avenue Chatswood – Drainage/Flooding

rules.development_sub_category: 10a–14 Boundary St, 57–63 William St, 118–138 Archer St Roseville – Vehicle Access
rules.rule_group: Part B 10a–14 Boundary St, 57–63 William St, 118–138 Archer St Roseville – Vehicle Access
rules.rule_title: 10a–14 Boundary St, 57–63 William St, 118–138 Archer St Roseville – Vehicle Access
rules.rule_text: No vehicle access is allowed from Boundary Street. All vehicle access from Archer Street must be from the William Street end of the site, to minimise conflict with vehicles queuing at the Boundary Street/Archer Street and Boundary Street/Pacific Highway intersections.
rules.applies_to: 10a–14 Boundary St, 57–63 William St, 118–138 Archer St Roseville – Vehicle Access

rules.development_sub_category: Roseville Site – Road Widening and Bicycle/Pedestrian Pathway
rules.rule_group: Part B Roseville Site – Road Widening and Bicycle/Pedestrian Pathway
rules.rule_title: Roseville Site – Road Widening and Bicycle/Pedestrian Pathway
rules.rule_text: To assist with traffic movement and allow adequate space for bicycle/pedestrian pathways, all new developments must provide road widening and pathway dedications (specific dimensions in image files). [controls in image files – not extracted]
rules.applies_to: Roseville Site – Road Widening and Bicycle/Pedestrian Pathway

rules.rule_group: Part B Roseville Site – Drainage/Flooding
rules.rule_text: Any new development must submit a flood study to identify the existing flood extent and show the development will not adversely affect adjoining properties. A clear overland flood path must be maintained.

rules.rule_group: Part B 59 William Street Roseville – Heritage Setbacks
rules.rule_text: To protect the significance of the heritage item at 59 William Street, specific setbacks apply (dimensions in image files). Under Clause 6.10 of Willoughby LEP, the minimum site area for any future development is 4,000m². [controls in image files – not extracted]
rules.extraction_note: setback dimensions in image files | Supplemented by numeric controls in merged rules.

rules.development_sub_category: Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Side Setback Controls
rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Side Setback Controls
rules.rule_title: Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Side Setback Controls
rules.rule_text: Side setback controls for R2 zone (by building height): Up to 24m height: 1.5m minimum side setback; >24m–36m height: (height − 4.5m)/4 + 1.5m; >36m: up to 8.5m, minimum 2.5m. Excludes common side boundary walls for attached dual occupancies.
rules.applies_to: Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Side Setback Controls

rules.development_sub_category: Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Rear Setback Controls (by lot area and height)
rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Rear Setback Controls (by lot area and height)
rules.rule_title: Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Rear Setback Controls (by lot area and height)
rules.applies_to: Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Rear Setback Controls (by lot area and height)

rules.development_sub_category: Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Secondary Road Setback (corner lots)
rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Secondary Road Setback (corner lots)
rules.rule_title: Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Secondary Road Setback (corner lots)
rules.rule_text: Secondary road setback (corner lots) R2 zone: 700–900m² = 2m; 900–1500m² = 3m; >1500m² = 5m.
rules.applies_to: Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Secondary Road Setback (corner lots)

rules.development_sub_category: Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Dual Occupancy Width Requirements
rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Dual Occupancy Width Requirements
rules.rule_title: Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Dual Occupancy Width Requirements
rules.rule_text: Each dwelling must have a minimum width (measured at the building line) of: (a) 6.5m where car space is accessed from the primary road; or (b) 5m in any other case. The maximum height of the floor level is 4m. If more than 2m above existing ground level, must be set back a minimum 3m from the boundary. Area above 2m not to exceed 12m².
rules.applies_to: Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Dual Occupancy Width Requirements

rules.development_sub_category: Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Lot Width for Dual Occupancy
rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Lot Width for Dual Occupancy
rules.rule_title: Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Lot Width for Dual Occupancy
rules.rule_text: The width of the parent lot must not be less than: (a) 15m; or (b) 12m where car parking is provided at the rear of the lot or in a basement accessed only from a secondary road, parallel road or laneway.
rules.applies_to: Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Lot Width for Dual Occupancy

rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 1 – R2 Zone Side Setback (manor/attached/multi)
rules.rule_text: Minimum side boundary setback is 1.5m. If the development is more than 10m behind the building line and more than 4.5m above existing ground level, the minimum side setback is: s = h − 3m (where s is minimum setback and h is height of any part of the building).

rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 2 – R3 Zone Side Setback
rules.rule_text: Minimum side boundary setback is 1.5m. If development is more than 10m behind the building line and more than 4.5m above existing ground level, minimum setback: s = h − 3m.

rules.development_sub_category: Attachment 2 – R3 Zone Secondary Road Setback
rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 2 – R3 Zone Secondary Road Setback
rules.rule_title: Attachment 2 – R3 Zone Secondary Road Setback

rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 2 – R3 Zone Street Setback

rules.rule_title: Attachment 2 – R3 Zone Setback from Boundary (small elements)

rules.rule_text: Minimum private open space area: 1 bedroom or studio = 8m²; 2 bedrooms = 12m²; 3 or more bedrooms = 16m².

rules.development_sub_category: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Communal Open Space

rules.applies_to: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Communal Open Space

rules.rule_text: Minimum width and depth of open space areas (areas with less than the minimum dimension are excluded): 1.5m.

rules.development_sub_category: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Landscaping per Lot

rules.rule_title: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Landscaping per Lot
rules.rule_text: Minimum landscaping per potential lot: 30%.
rules.applies_to: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Landscaping per Lot

rules.development_sub_category: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Deep Soil in Landscaped Area
rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Deep Soil in Landscaped Area
rules.rule_title: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Deep Soil in Landscaped Area
rules.rule_text: Minimum 50% of the landscaped area must be deep soil.
rules.applies_to: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Deep Soil in Landscaped Area

rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Landscaping Forward of Building Line
rules.rule_title: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Landscaping Forward of Building Line
rules.rule_text: Minimum 50% of the area forward of the building line must be landscaped.
rules.applies_to: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Landscaping Forward of Building Line

rules.development_sub_category: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Front Setback
rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Front Setback
rules.rule_title: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Front Setback
rules.rule_text: Minimum front setback: 6m.
rules.applies_to: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Front Setback

rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Secondary Street Setback
rules.rule_title: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Secondary Street Setback

rules.applies_to: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Secondary Street Setback

rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Side Setback

rules.rule_text: Minimum side setback: 1.5m for 1 and 2 storey dwellings + 1.2m for each level above 2 storeys. The total setback applies to each level above the second storey.
rules.applies_to: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Side Setback

rules.development_sub_category: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Rear Setback by Storey
rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Rear Setback by Storey
rules.rule_title: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Rear Setback by Storey
rules.rule_text: Minimum rear setback: one storey = 6m; two storeys = 8m; three storeys = 10m.
rules.applies_to: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Rear Setback by Storey

rules.development_sub_category: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Garage Setback from Boundary
rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Garage Setback from Boundary
rules.rule_title: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Garage Setback from Boundary
rules.rule_text: Minimum garage setback from boundary: 3m.

rules.development_sub_category: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Boundary Wall
rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Boundary Wall
rules.rule_title: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Boundary Wall

rules.applies_to: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Boundary Wall

rules.development_sub_category: Attachment 2 – R3 Multi Dwelling – Secondary Road/Laneway Setback
rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 2 – R3 Multi Dwelling – Secondary Road/Laneway Setback
rules.rule_title: Attachment 2 – R3 Multi Dwelling – Secondary Road/Laneway Setback

rules.applies_to: Attachment 2 – R3 Multi Dwelling – Secondary Road/Laneway Setback

rules.development_sub_category: Attachment 2 – R3 Multi Dwelling – Setback from Rear Boundary
rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 2 – R3 Multi Dwelling – Setback from Rear Boundary
rules.rule_title: Attachment 2 – R3 Multi Dwelling – Setback from Rear Boundary
rules.rule_text: Minimum setback from rear boundary: 1m.
rules.applies_to: Attachment 2 – R3 Multi Dwelling – Setback from Rear Boundary

rules.development_sub_category: Attachment 2 – R3 Multi Dwelling – Private Open Space
rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 2 – R3 Multi Dwelling – Private Open Space
rules.rule_title: Attachment 2 – R3 Multi Dwelling – Private Open Space
rules.rule_text: Minimum private open space per dwelling: 16m².
rules.applies_to: Attachment 2 – R3 Multi Dwelling – Private Open Space

rules.development_sub_category: Attachment 2 – R3 Multi Dwelling – Communal Area Setback
rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 2 – R3 Multi Dwelling – Communal Area Setback
rules.rule_title: Attachment 2 – R3 Multi Dwelling – Communal Area Setback
rules.rule_text: Minimum communal area setback: 3m.
rules.applies_to: Attachment 2 – R3 Multi Dwelling – Communal Area Setback

rules.development_sub_category: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Adaptable Dwellings (%)
rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Adaptable Dwellings (%)
rules.rule_title: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Adaptable Dwellings (%)
rules.rule_text: Minimum percentage of adaptable dwellings: single storey = 10%; two storey = 25%; three storey = 33%; four storey = 50%.
rules.applies_to: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Adaptable Dwellings (%)

rules.development_sub_category: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Ceiling Heights
rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Ceiling Heights
rules.rule_title: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Ceiling Heights
rules.rule_text: Minimum ceiling heights: habitable rooms = 2.7m; non-habitable rooms = 2.4m.
rules.applies_to: Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Ceiling Heights

rules.rule_group: Part B Attachment 2 – R3 Attached Dwellings – Glazing
rules.rule_text: Minimum glass area not less than 10% of (refer to image for complete specification). [controls in image files – not extracted]
rules.extraction_note: full glazing control in image files

rules.development_group: Part C – C4 Environmental Living Zone
rules.rule_group: Part C Part C – Overview
rules.rule_text: Part C provides specific performance criteria and controls for low impact development on land close to foreshore areas and environmentally sensitive locations in the C4 Environmental Living Zone. Further controls apply to C4 zone developments within a heritage conservation area (Part H prevails). [controls in image files – not extracted]
rules.extraction_note: detailed performance criteria and controls in image files

rules.rule_group: Part D Part D – Overview
rules.rule_text: Part D includes performance criteria and controls for all types of commercial and non-residential development, including the commercial components of shop top housing and mixed use developments and place based plans. Heritage controls (Part H) may also apply. [controls in image files – not extracted]
permissible_uses: Take Away Food and Drink Premises
rules.extraction_note: detailed controls predominantly in image files

rules.development_sub_category: Centre-Based Child Care Facilities – Commercial Controls
rules.rule_group: Part D Centre-Based Child Care Facilities – Commercial Controls
rules.rule_title: Centre-Based Child Care Facilities – Commercial Controls
rules.rule_text: Section 7.3 of Part D includes provisions for centre-based child care facilities. These controls also apply to child care facilities in residential areas (with additional controls in Part B, Section 6).
rules.applies_to: Centre-Based Child Care Facilities – Commercial Controls
permissible_uses: Take Away Food and Drink Premises

rules.rule_group: Part E Part E – Overview
rules.rule_text: Part E includes guidelines and controls for industrial development and other land uses permitted in the industrial zones. [controls in image files – not extracted]

rules.development_group: Part F – Transport and Parking Management
rules.rule_group: Part F Part F – Overview
rules.rule_text: Part F applies to all developments. It provides advice and controls for traffic management, off-street car parking, bicycle parking and end-of-trip facilities, loading and unloading facilities, and other traffic and transport related matters. Informed by Willoughby Integrated Transport Strategy 2036.

rules.development_group: Part F – Transport and Parking Management
rules.rule_group: Part F Car Parking Numbers
rules.rule_text: The required number of car parking spaces is provided in Part F (Transport and Parking Management). Except for the number of spaces, the Housing Code (Codes SEPP, Part 3) requirements for car parking and vehicle access apply in the R2 zone.

rules.rule_group: Part G Part G – Overview
rules.rule_text: Part G applies to any development that involves the removal of trees or vegetation. Made under Part 2.2 of State Environmental Planning Policy (Biodiversity and Conservation) 2021. Supported by Willoughby Vegetation Management Guidelines.

rules.rule_group: Part H Part H – Overview
rules.rule_text: Part H is subject to Clause 5.10 (Heritage conservation) of Willoughby LEP. It includes controls to conserve and protect the heritage values of existing dwellings and commercial/retail buildings, and ensures new development is in keeping with heritage conservation area character. Where inconsistent with Parts B, C and D, Part H controls prevail.

rules.rule_group: Part I Part I – Overview
rules.rule_text: Part I provides advice and controls for stormwater management. Applies to proposals that will increase impervious surface areas and that need to provide an onsite detention (OSD) system and/or rainwater reuse tank.

rules.rule_group: Part J Part J – Overview
rules.rule_text: Part J provides advice and controls to achieve energy efficient and environmentally sustainable buildings. Applies to any application for new development or significant alterations and additions to ensure best practice sustainability measures are included.

rules.development_group: Part K – Lane Cove Tunnel Ventilation Stacks
rules.rule_group: Part K Part K – Overview
rules.rule_text: Part K applies to any new development within an 800m radius of the ventilation stacks in Sirius Road, Lane Cove West and Marsden Street, Artarmon.

rules.rule_group: Part L Part L – Overview
rules.rule_text: Part L provides specific guidelines and controls for development in the Chatswood CBD and local centres: Artarmon, Castlecrag, North Willoughby High Street, Naremburn, Northbridge, Penshurst Street, and Willoughby South. Also includes controls for specific sites. Where inconsistent with Parts B and D, Part L controls prevail.

rules.rule_group: Part L Artarmon Local Centre
rules.rule_text: Artarmon local centre: Hampden Road and Wilkes Plaza, adjacent to Artarmon railway station. Development opportunities for residential accommodation in shop top housing. Controls maintain fine grain shopfronts and retain heritage values of Artarmon Heritage Conservation Area.

rules.rule_group: Part L Castlecrag Local Centre
rules.rule_text: Castlecrag local centre: Edinburgh Road between Eastern Valley Way and Rutland Avenue/The Postern. Development opportunities for shop top housing and residential flat buildings at 95–103 Edinburgh Road. Must respect heritage values of Griffin Heritage Conservation Area.

rules.rule_group: Part L North Willoughby Local Centre
rules.rule_text: North Willoughby local centre: Around intersection of Penshurst Street and Victoria Avenue, extending south to Patrick Street. Development opportunities for residential accommodation in shop top housing.
rules.source_ref: Part L – North Willoughby Local Centre

rules.rule_group: Part L High Street Local Centre
rules.rule_text: High Street local centre: Eastern side of High Street, bounded by McClelland Street and Glover Street. Development opportunities for shop top housing. Controls for upper level setbacks at rear to protect residential amenity of adjoining dwellings.
rules.source_ref: Part L – High Street Local Centre

rules.rule_group: Part L Naremburn Local Centre
rules.rule_text: Naremburn local centre: Western side of Willoughby Road, bounded by Quiamong, Bongalong and Glenmore Streets. Includes two heritage items. Shop top housing opportunities. Development must maintain fine grain shopfronts and respect heritage items at 284/284A and 272–276 Willoughby Road.

rules.rule_group: Part L Northbridge Local Centre
rules.rule_text: Northbridge local centre: Along Sailors Bay Road, Eastern Valley Way and Strathallen Avenue. Shop top housing and residential flat building opportunities on southern side of Sailors Bay Road and rear of Northbridge Plaza.

rules.rule_group: Part L Penshurst Street Local Centre
rules.rule_text: Penshurst Street local centre: Along and north of Mowbray Road. Mix of business premises, shop top housing and small retail. Controls include upper level setbacks at rear to protect residential amenity of adjoining dwellings.
rules.source_ref: Part L – Penshurst Street Local Centre

rules.rule_group: Part L Willoughby South Local Centre
rules.rule_text: Willoughby South local centre: Fronts Willoughby Road with additional commercial areas branching off Frenchs Road. Development opportunities for shop top housing and residential flat buildings.
rules.source_ref: Part L – Willoughby South Local Centre

rules.rule_group: Part L Chatswood CBD
rules.rule_text: The Chatswood CBD Planning and Urban Design Strategy to 2036 guides future development over 20 years. The CBD includes commercial/office buildings, civic buildings, library and performance facilities, major retail complexes, banking, cafes, restaurants, private health facilities and schools. Part L allows some additional residential growth as part of mixed use development in Chatswood CBD.

rules.development_sub_category: Storage Areas and Plant Rooms – Maximum GFA
rules.rule_group: Part B Storage Areas and Plant Rooms – Maximum GFA
rules.rule_title: Storage Areas and Plant Rooms – Maximum GFA
rules.rule_text: The combined below ground floor storage areas and plant/mechanical service rooms shall have a maximum gross floor area of 5% of the total allowable floor area for a dwelling house.

rules.rule_group: Part B Minimum Landscaped Area
rules.rule_text: Minimum landscaped area for site area Under 200 m² is 0.25 × site area.
rules.extraction_note: Includes deep soil zones and soft landscaped areas (min 1m dimension)

rules.rule_group: Part B Minimum Landscaped Area
rules.rule_text: Minimum landscaped area for site area 200–400 m² is (0.35 × site area) − 20.
rules.extraction_note: Includes deep soil zones and soft landscaped areas (min 1m dimension)

rules.rule_group: Part B Minimum Landscaped Area
rules.rule_text: Minimum landscaped area for site area 401–600 m² is (0.6 × site area) − 120.
rules.extraction_note: Includes deep soil zones and soft landscaped areas (min 1m dimension)

rules.rule_group: Part B Minimum Landscaped Area
rules.rule_text: Minimum landscaped area for site area 601–1000 m² is (0.525 × site area) − 75.
rules.extraction_note: Includes deep soil zones and soft landscaped areas (min 1m dimension)

rules.rule_group: Part B Minimum Landscaped Area
rules.rule_text: Minimum landscaped area for site area 1001–1500 m² is (0.6 × site area) − 150.
rules.extraction_note: Includes deep soil zones and soft landscaped areas (min 1m dimension)

rules.rule_group: Part B Minimum Landscaped Area
rules.rule_text: Minimum landscaped area for site area Over 1500 m² is 0.5 × site area.
rules.extraction_note: Includes deep soil zones and soft landscaped areas (min 1m dimension)

rules.rule_group: Part B Minimum Landscaped Area – Dual Occupancy
rules.rule_text: A minimum landscaped area of 35% distributed proportionately for each dwelling and potential future subdivision boundaries is required for dual occupancy.

rules.rule_group: Part B Street Frontage Landscaping – Site ≥18m wide
rules.rule_text: Landscaping at street frontage must be not less than 70% of the area forward of the building line to the primary road where site width at building line is at least 18m.
rules.value_unit: % of area forward of building line

rules.rule_group: Part B Street Frontage Landscaping – Site <18m wide
rules.rule_text: Landscaping at street frontage must be not less than 50% of the area forward of the building line to the primary road where site width at building line is less than 18m.
rules.value_unit: % of area forward of building line

rules.rule_group: Part B Tree Clearance from Structures
rules.rule_text: A minimum clearance of 3m must be maintained between proposed structures and trees, measured from the outside base of the tree trunk.

rules.development_sub_category: Minimum Lot Size – Manor Houses, Attached Dwellings, Multi Dwelling Housing, RFBs
rules.rule_group: Part B Minimum Lot Size – Manor Houses, Attached Dwellings, Multi Dwelling Housing, RFBs
rules.rule_title: Minimum Lot Size – Manor Houses, Attached Dwellings, Multi Dwelling Housing, RFBs
rules.rule_text: Minimum lot size for manor houses, attached dwellings, multi dwelling housing and residential flat buildings in R3 and R4 zones is 1100m².
rules.source_ref: Clause 6.10 Willoughby LEP / Part B s4.3.1(a)
rules.applies_to: Manor house, Attached dwelling, Multi dwelling housing, Residential flat building

rules.rule_group: Part B Minimum Street Frontage – Major Developments
rules.rule_text: The minimum street frontage for manor houses, attached dwellings, multi dwelling housing and residential flat buildings is 27m.
rules.applies_to: Manor house, Attached dwelling, Multi dwelling housing, Residential flat building

rules.rule_group: Part B Adaptable Housing – Minimum Percentage
rules.rule_text: Minimum 10% adaptable dwellings required for Single storey dwellings.
rules.applies_to: Manor house, Attached dwelling, Multi dwelling housing, Residential flat building, Shop top housing, Mixed use
rules.extraction_note: Must be designed to Class C standard per AS 4299-1995; Class A/B encouraged

rules.rule_group: Part B Adaptable Housing – Minimum Percentage
rules.rule_text: Minimum 25% adaptable dwellings required for Two storey buildings.
rules.applies_to: Manor house, Attached dwelling, Multi dwelling housing, Residential flat building, Shop top housing, Mixed use
rules.extraction_note: Must be designed to Class C standard per AS 4299-1995; Class A/B encouraged

rules.rule_group: Part B Adaptable Housing – Minimum Percentage
rules.rule_text: Minimum 33% adaptable dwellings required for Three storey buildings.
rules.applies_to: Manor house, Attached dwelling, Multi dwelling housing, Residential flat building, Shop top housing, Mixed use
rules.extraction_note: Must be designed to Class C standard per AS 4299-1995; Class A/B encouraged

rules.rule_group: Part B Adaptable Housing – Minimum Percentage
rules.rule_text: Minimum 50% adaptable dwellings required for RFBs greater than 3 storeys.
rules.applies_to: Manor house, Attached dwelling, Multi dwelling housing, Residential flat building, Shop top housing, Mixed use
rules.extraction_note: Must be designed to Class C standard per AS 4299-1995; Class A/B encouraged
rules.condition: Building type: RFBs greater than 3 storeys

rules.rule_group: Part B Adaptable Housing – Minimum Percentage
rules.rule_text: Minimum 50% adaptable dwellings required for Mixed use and shop top housing (with lift access).
rules.applies_to: Manor house, Attached dwelling, Multi dwelling housing, Residential flat building, Shop top housing, Mixed use
rules.extraction_note: Must be designed to Class C standard per AS 4299-1995; Class A/B encouraged
rules.condition: Building type: Mixed use and shop top housing (with lift access)

rules.rule_group: Part B Roof SRI – Pitch <15°
rules.rule_text: For roof pitch less than 15°, the 3-year Solar Reflectance Index (SRI) minimum is 64.

rules.rule_group: Part B Roof SRI – Pitch >15°
rules.rule_text: For roof pitch greater than 15°, the 3-year Solar Reflectance Index (SRI) minimum is 34.

rules.rule_group: Part B Roof SRI – Terrace Areas
rules.rule_text: For terrace areas, the 3-year Solar Reflectance Index (SRI) minimum is 38.

rules.rule_group: Part B Site Coverage – Residential Flat Buildings
rules.rule_text: Site coverage for a 3 storey residential flat building must not exceed 30% of the site area.

rules.rule_group: Part B Site Coverage – Residential Flat Buildings
rules.rule_text: Site coverage for a 4 storey residential flat building must not exceed 28% of the site area.

rules.rule_group: Part B Site Coverage – Residential Flat Buildings
rules.rule_text: Site coverage for a 5 storey residential flat building must not exceed 26% of the site area.

rules.rule_group: Part B Site Coverage – Residential Flat Buildings
rules.rule_text: Site coverage for a 6 storey residential flat building must not exceed 24% of the site area.

rules.rule_group: Part B Site Coverage – Residential Flat Buildings
rules.rule_text: Site coverage for a 7 storey residential flat building must not exceed 22% of the site area.

rules.rule_group: Part B Site Coverage – Residential Flat Buildings
rules.rule_text: Site coverage for a 8 or more storeys residential flat building must not exceed 20% of the site area.

rules.development_sub_category: Front Setback – RFB in R3 zone
rules.rule_group: Part B Front Setback – RFB in R3 zone
rules.rule_title: Front Setback – RFB in R3 zone
rules.rule_text: Front setback for residential flat buildings in the R3 zone is 7.5m.

rules.development_sub_category: Front Setback – RFB in R4 zone
rules.rule_group: Part B Front Setback – RFB in R4 zone
rules.rule_title: Front Setback – RFB in R4 zone
rules.rule_text: Front setback for residential flat buildings in the R4 zone is 9m.

rules.rule_group: Part B Side and Rear Setbacks – RFBs
rules.rule_text: Side and rear setbacks for residential flat buildings: 3m plus 1.2m per storey above ground floor level.
rules.extraction_note: Total required setback applies to all floors above the third floor

rules.development_sub_category: Corner Lot Setback – RFB in R3 zone
rules.rule_group: Part B Corner Lot Setback – RFB in R3 zone
rules.rule_title: Corner Lot Setback – RFB in R3 zone
rules.rule_text: Secondary road setback for residential flat buildings on corner lots in R3 zone is 3.5m.

rules.development_sub_category: Corner Lot Setback – RFB in R4 zone
rules.rule_group: Part B Corner Lot Setback – RFB in R4 zone
rules.rule_title: Corner Lot Setback – RFB in R4 zone
rules.rule_text: Secondary road setback for residential flat buildings on corner lots in R4 zone: 3m plus 1.2m per storey above ground floor.

rules.development_sub_category: Soft Landscaped Area / Deep Soil Zone – RFBs ≥3 storeys
rules.rule_group: Part B Soft Landscaped Area / Deep Soil Zone – RFBs ≥3 storeys
rules.rule_title: Soft Landscaped Area / Deep Soil Zone – RFBs ≥3 storeys
rules.rule_text: For residential accommodation of 3 or more storeys and 4 or more dwellings, the site must comprise at least 35% of soft landscaped areas and deep soil zones at ground level (excluding planter boxes).
rules.applies_to: Residential flat building, Shop top housing, Mixed use
rules.value_unit: % of site area at ground level

rules.rule_group: Part B Communal Open Space – Per Dwelling
rules.rule_text: For developments with 15 or more apartments, provide 25m² of communal open space per dwelling where balconies are the only form of open space.
rules.applies_to: Residential flat building, Shop top housing, Mixed use
rules.condition: 15+ apartments; balconies only form of open space

rules.rule_group: Part B Communal Open Space – Minimum Dimension
rules.rule_text: Communal open space must have a minimum dimension of 5m and minimum area of 50m².
rules.applies_to: Residential flat building, Shop top housing, Mixed use

rules.rule_group: Part B Children's Play Area
rules.rule_text: For developments with 15 or more apartments, communal open space must include a children's play area with minimum area of 30m², minimum dimension of 5m, and grade no steeper than 1 in 20.
rules.applies_to: Residential flat building, Shop top housing, Mixed use

rules.rule_group: Part B Maximum FSR – R2 Zone (Area 1)
rules.rule_text: Maximum floor space ratio for lot area ≤200 m² in R2 zone (Area 1) is 0.65:1.
rules.source_ref: Clause 4.4A Willoughby LEP / Part B Att.1

rules.rule_group: Part B Maximum FSR – R2 Zone (Area 1)
rules.rule_text: Maximum floor space ratio for lot area >200–≤300 m² in R2 zone (Area 1) is 0.55:1.
rules.source_ref: Clause 4.4A Willoughby LEP / Part B Att.1

rules.rule_group: Part B Maximum FSR – R2 Zone (Area 1)
rules.rule_text: Maximum floor space ratio for lot area >300–≤400 m² in R2 zone (Area 1) is 0.50:1.
rules.source_ref: Clause 4.4A Willoughby LEP / Part B Att.1

rules.rule_group: Part B Maximum FSR – R2 Zone (Area 1)
rules.rule_text: Maximum floor space ratio for lot area >400–≤500 m² in R2 zone (Area 1) is 0.47:1.
rules.source_ref: Clause 4.4A Willoughby LEP / Part B Att.1

rules.rule_group: Part B Maximum FSR – R2 Zone (Area 1)
rules.rule_text: Maximum floor space ratio for lot area >500–≤600 m² in R2 zone (Area 1) is 0.45:1.
rules.source_ref: Clause 4.4A Willoughby LEP / Part B Att.1

rules.rule_group: Part B Maximum FSR – R2 Zone (Area 1)
rules.rule_text: Maximum floor space ratio for lot area >600–≤700 m² in R2 zone (Area 1) is 0.43:1.
rules.source_ref: Clause 4.4A Willoughby LEP / Part B Att.1

rules.rule_group: Part B Maximum FSR – R2 Zone (Area 1)
